summary: Joel Spolsky outlines Fog Creek Software's plans for 2003, including a printed column for Programmer's Paradise, a second book on management, server migration, new office space, and product updates for CityDesk and FogBUGZ. He emphasizes customer-driven development for CityDesk, focusing on removing specific blockers that prevent adoption. For FogBUGZ, the strategy involves commoditizing complements by supporting open-source databases and expanding into broader development workflow management.
key_points: Customer feedback should drive feature prioritization: focus on removing the specific 'I can't use it because X' blockers rather than speculating about neat features
Fog Creek is seeking office space with windowed private offices, reflecting Joel's strong stance that developers need natural light and private workspaces
FogBUGZ strategy involves commoditizing complements by supporting open-source databases instead of requiring expensive Microsoft SQL Server
Joel candidly admits that much of his 2003 writing will be 'rampant speculation, disguised as hard facts'
money_quotes: I think I've already told you everything I know, so most of what I'll write about during 2003 will be rampant speculation, disguised as hard facts!
Overcoming these x's is the easiest way to improve sales.

The great thing about having customers and listening to them is that we get this kind of information, and we don't have to speculate about what features would be neat, and we don't do things just because some programmer thinks they would be fun.

I ask them how they would feel about living in an apartment where the bedroom didn't have a window. 'You would freak out, right?' And that's for a room you're going to sleep in, at night. For a room where you spend the day it's obviously non-negotiable.
I don't have a technical description of cool. 'I know it when I see it.'
key_insight: The most effective product strategy is listening to customers who say 'I like your product but I can't use it because X' and systematically eliminating those blockers.
